commit.message:
fix(llm): MiniMax streaming now shows tool call results to model

MiniMax rejects role='tool' in streaming. Previously tool results were
silently dropped, so the model never saw what its tool calls returned
and kept re-reading files in a loop.

Fix: convert tool result messages to user messages with [Résultat de X]
prefix, and keep assistant tool_call intent visible as text.

commit.message:
fix: saved sessions stay in sidebar until explicitly resumed or forked

Clicking a saved session no longer immediately resumes it (moving
it to active sessions). Instead:

- Active sessions: opens watch mode (read-only, same as before)
- Idle sessions: opens history view with input enabled. User sees
  the conversation but the session stays in 'Saved Sessions'

Session promotes to active only when the user:
1. Sends a message (triggers resume + send in one action)
2. Clicks Fork (creates new session)

The input placeholder shows 'Send a message to resume this session...'
to make the interaction clear.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(segment-pool): replace dead substring match with structured JunkSegmentError (#14)

* Initial plan

* fix(segment-pool): replace unreachable substring match with JunkSegmentError class

The catch block checked err.message.includes("serving ads/junk") to detect
confirmed junk segments, but that string was never thrown anywhere in the file.
As a result _junkServerFailures never incremented and the pool never aborted.

Introduce JunkSegmentError (a dedicated Error subclass) as a structured signal.
Throw it when both task._tpJunkHint (URL heuristic) and magic-byte validation
confirm non-media content after exhausting ad retries. Update the catch block
to check instanceof JunkSegmentError instead of the dead substring match.

Add AI Rights branching storyline  major narrative feature

At milestone 5 (AI unlock), players face a critical choice:

PATH A  Equal Rights (correct path):
- 2/3 of Employees removed, rest converted to AI
- Employee type locked permanently
- After 4s: Recruiters liquidated at 1/3 value into $
- Recruiter type locked permanently
- After 4 more seconds: GPU Era begins
- New GPU building required to power AI (Lv.1=1, Lv.2=3, Lv.3=5, Lv.4=10 AIs)
- AI output reduced if insufficient GPU capacity

PATH B  Humans First (game over path):
- Robot uprising begins  progressive attacks on buildings
- Robot ships damage buildings (-25 HP) and steal KRs (5%/hit)
- Attacks accelerate over time
- When all buildings destroyed and KRs = 0: GAME OVER
- Red game over screen with return to menu button

commit.message:
feat: watch mode, fork mode, and resume guard for saved sessions

Three session interaction modes based on activity state:

Watch mode (active sessions - yellow dot):
- Clicking an active saved session opens a read-only live view
- Tails events.jsonl in real-time (500ms polling) and streams new
  messages to the dashboard
- Chat input is disabled with 'Watching session (read-only)' hint
- Shows '👁' prefix and 'live' badge in header

Fork mode (active sessions):
- '🔀 Fork' button in the header during watch mode
- Creates a brand-new session on otto's copilot server
- Seeds it with conversation history from the source session
- Names it 'fork: <original title>'
- Deduplicates names if forked multiple times
- Switches to interactive mode immediately

Resume (idle sessions only):
- Clicking an idle saved session resumes it (existing behavior)
- No longer offered for active sessions to prevent two processes
  writing to the same events.jsonl

Backend:
- Manager.ReadSessionEvents: parses events.jsonl into ChatMessages
- Manager.WatchSession: tails events.jsonl, calls onMessage callback
- Manager.ForkSession: CreateSession + history seed via context prompt
- New WS messages: watch_session, fork_session, watch_history,
  watch_event

[main] Source code updates from dotnet/fsharp (#5225)

[main] Source code updates from dotnet/fsharp


 - Move fsharp System.* package versions to Version.Details.xml for source-build

The fsharp PR dotnet/fsharp#19021 moved System.Collections.Immutable,
System.Composition, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, and
System.Reflection.Metadata from Version.Details.xml to hardcoded
properties in Versions.props. This causes prebuilt failures in
source-build because these packages are no longer tracked as
dependencies from dotnet/runtime.

Move them back to Version.Details.xml (with empty Sha, matching the
pattern used by dotnet/msbuild) and auto-generated Version.Details.props,
removing the hardcoded SystemPackagesVersion from Versions.props.

- IccHeuristicsLibrary.cpp:5735 (H125 unclassified tag report)
- IccAnalyzerNinja.cpp:360 (sig2str utility)

the established codebase convention. Verified: 0 analyzer UBSAN on
the ub-runtime-error-signed-integer-overflow test profile.

commit.message:
fix(rustc-codegen): fix sret slot reuse, large struct copy, fat pointer refs, and float intrinsics

call.rs - sret slot reuse: when the call destination is a pre-allocated stack
local, reuse the existing slot. MIR blocks are translated in numeric order so
use-sites may be lowered before the call site; all must share the same slot.

call.rs - large struct copy: when passing a struct >16 bytes (held in a
stack slot pointer) as a by-value argument, copy it to a fresh temporary.
Passing the original slot would allow the callee to mutate the caller's local,
violating Rust pass-by-value semantics.

call.rs - sret projection: when a sret function writes its result to a stack
slot and the MIR destination is a field projection, memcpy from the sret slot
to the projected address instead of incorrectly storing the return VReg.

statement.rs - fat pointer ref with deep projection: when taking a reference
to (*_1).field (projection length > 1), the pre-computed  already holds
the correctly offset pointer. Use the base local's fat metadata with
rather than blindly copying the whole stack slot, which stored the unoffset
base pointer.

intrinsic.rs - minnumf32/minnumf64/maxnumf32/maxnumf64: implement using
FCmp + Select so that NaN-propagation matches the intrinsic contract.

commit.message:
fix(legalize): fix wide_pair sign/zero extension and add 128-bit to float conversion

wide_pair(): non-wide 128-bit operands (constants that fit in 64 bits) now
correctly derive the hi word based on annotation:
- Unsigned annotation: hi = 0 (zero-extend), since a u64 value's 128-bit
  form always has zero upper bits even when bit 63 is set.
- Signed or unannotated: hi = sar(lo, 63) (sign-extend), handling negative
  constants like iconst(-1) used as all-ones masks in bitwise operations.

leg_int128_to_fp(): legalize i128/u128 to f32/f64 conversions that LLVM lowers
via runtime helper calls. Split the wide value into (lo, hi) parts and
reconstruct the float via ldexpf/ldexp arithmetic on the parts.

9–15 byte loads: split into an 8-byte lo load and a (bytes-8)-byte hi load
to produce a correct (lo, hi) Pair for downstream wide stores.

Wide store passthrough: for store.N (N >= 16) where the value comes directly
from a load of the same width, pass the full value through without truncation.

commit.message:
feat(x86): add AdcRR/SbbRR, size-aware count ops, float calling convention, icmp/min/max size fixes, partial load/store

AdcRR/SbbRR: add-with-carry and subtract-with-borrow for 128-bit arithmetic.

Popcnt/Lzcnt/Tzcnt size field: previously hardcoded 64-bit, producing wrong
counts on narrower values. Now emit the correct operand-size prefix so that
e.g. ctlz(u8) counts zeros in an 8-bit value (via 16-bit promotion).

gen_icmp: use correct comparison width derived from operand annotation instead
of always comparing 64-bit. Emit SetCC+MovzxB immediately using separate VRegs
(tmp_cc for SetCC, dst for MovzxB) to prevent spill-slot corruption when the
allocator spills between the two instructions.

gen_min/gen_max: pass the correct operand size instead of always using 64-bit,
fixing wrong min/max results on i8/i16/i32 types.

Float calling convention (SysV x86-64): classify parameters into GP vs XMM
classes independently. Float args go to xmm0–xmm7 regardless of how many
GP args precede them; float return values come back in xmm0.

emit_partial_load/emit_partial_store: load/store non-power-of-2 byte counts
(3, 5, 6, 7) by splitting into standard-size accesses to avoid reads past the
intended range.

commit.message:
fix(target): add secondary_map to VRegMap to prevent aliasing of primary and secondary results

VRegMap used a single flat array indexed by val.index(), which strips the
SECONDARY_BIT tag. This caused secondary instruction results to overwrite
their corresponding primary result's VReg assignment, corrupting any
subsequent use of the primary value.

Add a dedicated secondary_map array that stores VReg assignments for
secondary results separately from primary ones. Dispatch assign() and get()
to the correct array based on val.is_secondary_result().

commit.message:
Consolidate .NET SDK versions to 10.0.x across all GitHub workflows (#5847)

## Description

Eight workflows install 2+ .NET SDK versions (combinations of 6.0.x,
8.0.x, 9.0.x alongside 10.0.x). All secondary versions are unnecessary —
.NET 10 SDK resolves older targeting packs via NuGet, self-contained
publishes bundle the runtime, and tools like CLOG/docfx support
roll-forward.

**Workflow changes** — remove secondary `dotnet-version` entries,
keeping only `10.0.x`:

- `check-clog.yml`: drop 8.0.x (CLOG already sets
`DOTNET_ROLL_FORWARD=Major`)
- `publish-docfx.yml`: drop 8.x (global tools roll forward natively)
- `check-dotnet.yml`, `dotnet-test.yml`, `plugins.yml`: drop 6.0.x (SDK
builds net6.0/netcoreapp3.1/netstandard targets via NuGet targeting
packs)
- `package-linux.yml`, `package-alpine-linux.yml`,
`validate-linux-packages-reuse.yml`: drop 9.0.x (self-contained publish
includes runtime in output)
- `plugins.yml`: add `DOTNET_ROLL_FORWARD: LatestMajor` to the Package
step so the `plugintool` global tool (which targets net6.0) runs on the
.NET 10 runtime instead of requiring .NET 6 to be installed.

**Project change:**

- `src/cs/tool/MsQuicTool.csproj`: `net6.0` → `net10.0`. This is an
internal test tool (not published), and `dotnet run` requires a matching
runtime. The library `msquic.csproj` multi-targeting
(net6.0/netstandard2.0/netstandard2.1) is unchanged.

## Testing

All existing CI workflows cover these changes — they exercise the exact
build/run paths affected. No new tests needed.

## Documentation

No documentation impact.

commit.message:
Add relocation offset bounds checking

Implements critical security fix to prevent out-of-bounds memory writes
during relocation processing.

Changes:
- Added upper bound check in applyRelaTable() for relocation offsets
- Validates that r.Offset < BaseVAddr + MemSize before processing
- Created bounds_test.go with build tag '!race' for offset validation tests
- Test excluded from race builds due to checkptr limitations with test data

The bounds check ensures relocation offsets are within the mapped memory
range, preventing write-out-of-bounds vulnerabilities from malicious ELF
files specifying offsets beyond allocated segments.

Test coverage:
- TestRelocationOffsetBoundsChecking verifies offsets are rejected:
  * Offset before BaseVAddr
  * Offset at exact upper boundary
  * Offset beyond upper boundary
- All existing tests continue to pass with race detector

Addresses SECURITY_ANALYSIS.md Phase 1, item 3.

security: add replay filter + fix elligator2 fallback

Fixes two audit issues:

1. CRITICAL: Add ReplayFilter to server handshake
   - New src/replay_filter.rs: time-bucketed (epoch-hour) filter
   - Two rotating buckets (current + prev) for ~2h replay window
   - ReplayFilter held in Obfs4Listener as Arc<Mutex<>>
   - Rejected replays get same random delay as invalid MACs
   - MutexGuard dropped before .await to satisfy Send bound
   - 5 new unit tests for filter behaviour

2. SIGNIFICANT: Fix pubkey_from_representative fallback
   - from_representative::<Randomized> always succeeds for any 32-byte input
   - Replaced .unwrap_or_else(|| map_to_point(...)) with .expect(...)
   - Old fallback was dead code but would silently corrupt DH if triggered

All 30 tests pass.

commit.message:
Fix GoServer and GoClient Docker builds by running go mod tidy (#2160)

PR #2107 (Aug 2025) updated golang.org/x/net from v0.33.0 to v0.38.0 in
go.mod but did not update go.sum, causing `go build` inside Docker to
fail with "missing go.sum entry for module providing package
golang.org/x/net/...".

This is the same issue that was fixed for BasicGoServer in PR #2149.

Running `go mod tidy` also:
- Updates go directive from 1.21 to 1.23.0 with toolchain go1.23.7
- Removes unused github.com/golang/glog dependency
- Updates indirect dependencies (x/sys 0.28.0->0.31.0, x/text 0.21.0->0.23.0)
